notes: selfco vault cultivate session (full report). (1) New /vault cultivate mode added to the vault schema and the core vault skill (core PR #144): the serendipity pass — orient on the log delta, read mutually-unlinked clusters together, weave contextual cross-links into existing pages first, author at most 2-3 new synthesis pages per run each citing 2+ distinct sources, and log a 'Considered, declined' list so restraint is auditable. Load-bearing Goodhart guardrail: an empty run is a success state — an unattended loop rewarded for output will manufacture filler. (2) The mode now runs daily and unattended on the selfco-box (ojfbot/selfco-box@096ba13): new 'selfco-box cultivate' CLI, vendored cultivate-procedure.md, launchd job com.ojfbot.selfco-box.cultivate at 05:30 with lint.py --suggest-links feeding structural candidates into each run; 74/74 tests green. (3) First manual pass (ojfbot/selfco bdfaf3a): two new synthesis pages — dive-competence-subsea-bridge (the owner's scuba-instructor expertise is a live evaluative + fieldwork asset for the undersea-datacenter and marine-monitoring research domains; every documented pod sits at diver depth) and competency-frameworks-three-instances (INCOSE SECF, dive-agency instructor assessment, and the F1 literacy layer are one artifact class; the owner is a working rubric assessor, which is direct design input for the SE competency engine). 13 existing pages cross-linked. (4) Queue work-through (ojfbot/selfco 6c77473): four parallel reviewer agents judged 33 suggested-link pairs reading both pages each — 20 applied with context phrases, 15 rejected with reasons; _suggested-links.md regenerated with resolved-pairs memory so the daily job never re-litigates. Meta-finding worth a blog beat: the June-10 fleet-governance pages (tier model, kill log) independently re-derived the business-strategy canon ingested four days earlier without citing it — confirming the vault's own prediction that reference-only knowledge goes unconsulted; the cultivate layer is the structural answer.
